transcript:
Rep: Hi Jane, thanks for taking the time today. I wanted to follow up on our last conversation about your team's reporting challenges.

Jane: Sure, thanks for calling. Yeah, we've been struggling with our current setup. It takes our team about 15 hours a week just to compile reports manually.

Rep: That's significant. What kind of impact is that having on the business?

Jane: Well, we're missing deadlines on strategic initiatives because the team is bogged down in manual work. Our VP of Operations flagged it in the last quarterly review.

Rep: Got it. And who would ultimately sign off on a solution like this?

Jane: That would be Sarah Chen, our CFO. She controls the budget for operational tools. I'd need to put together a business case for her.

Rep: Makes sense. What's your budget range for this kind of solution?

Jane: We haven't finalized a budget yet, but Sarah mentioned we have some discretionary funds in Q2, probably in the $50-80K range for the year.

Rep: That aligns well with our typical engagement. What criteria would you use to evaluate different solutions?

Jane: Honestly, I'm not sure yet. I think ease of integration with our existing Salesforce setup is critical, and we'd want to see a clear ROI within the first quarter.

Rep: Those are great criteria. Is there anyone else on your team who's been pushing for a solution like this?

Jane: Actually yes, Mike from our analytics team has been really vocal about this. He's been researching tools on his own and has a lot of credibility with Sarah.

Rep: That's great to hear. Would it be helpful if I set up a technical deep-dive with Mike to go through the integration details?

Jane: That would be perfect. He'd love that.

Rep: Excellent. One last thing — what's your timeline for making a decision? Is there any urgency driving this?

Jane: We'd ideally want something in place before Q3 planning starts in August. So we're looking at making a decision in the next 6-8 weeks.

Rep: That's very doable. I'll send over a proposal this week and coordinate with Mike for the technical session. Sound good?

Jane: Sounds great, thanks!
